response.justification: The candidate brings genuine breadth across systems, a long career (17+ years), and recent exposure to agentic tooling and MCP frameworks, but falls well short of the role's core bar. The AI work is limited to roughly two months at the current employer and reflects infrastructure-level tooling rather than recognized AI platform expertise. The role explicitly calls for a director-level IC who can advise strategic customers and shape cloud-AI product direction — a positioning the resume does not support. There is no evidence of cloud platform strategy work, customer advisory experience, or the kind of AI thought leadership the seniority level demands.
strengths: 17+ years of polyglot engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure clearly meets the minimum tenure requirement
Founder experience with GISCollective and open-source delivery demonstrate entrepreneurial initiative and independent ownership
Recent hands-on work with MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks shows emerging AI-adjacent technical contribution
concerns: AI expertise is very recent (~2 months) and scoped to developer tooling, not recognized platform or product-level AI leadership
No evidence of customer advisory, technical evangelism, or strategic influence at the director-level IC scope the role requires
No cloud platform expertise (e.g., cloud architecture, cloud-native AI products) and no advanced degree in an AI-related field

key_factors.factor: Seniority gap vs. director-level IC scope
Entrepreneurial breadth and recent agentic tooling exposure
